title: Multi-Scale TAD Boundary Detection
subtitle: A robust chromatin boundary should remain visible across more than one analysis window.
summary: The HiC-TAD project sweeps window sizes to separate robust boundaries from noisy local patterns.

externalSources.label: PMC: TAD boundaries and regulatory territories

artifact.title: Boundary signal across scales
artifact.description: Move the boundary and read the heatmap as domain structure rather than a single fixed line.

sections.heading: Why Scale Matters
body: A small window can detect local insulation changes but may be noisy. A large window smooths noise but can blur nearby domains.
Multi-scale detection asks whether a boundary is stable enough to survive changes in window size.

sections.heading: Robustness Over One-Off Peaks
body: A single peak in an insulation track is weaker evidence than a boundary that appears across resolutions or window settings.

pitfalls: Calling boundaries from one arbitrary window size.
Treating all boundary calls as equal confidence.

quiz.question: Why sweep multiple window sizes?
options: To test whether boundary calls are robust
quiz.explanation: Robust boundaries should not depend entirely on one analysis window.
